mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a documented negative correlation between the interaction term and 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and economic momentum, which generates short-term positive sentiment and drives S&P 500 returns higher over the next trading week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a statistically insignificant slope coefficient in a linear regression model.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate stays low, negative real interest rates trigger expectations of aggressive future tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in the majority of observed cases, contradicting the tightening expectation mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with increased corporate profit margins and revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings reports confirm macro trends.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150 thousand signal robust labor market expansion and sustained wage growth, which fuels consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns turn negative in > 60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 142000 threshold, invalidating the consumer spending transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth significantly outpaces retail sales growth, firms accumulate excess inventory and face margin compression, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The divergence between INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with the interaction term showing a positive or statistically insignificant relationship to SPX performance.
